synthesis.summary: Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer pileup, previously attributed to a payload type-mismatch bug. This cycle Matteo surfaced a new, more fundamental root cause: the ClickHouse EBS volume's gp3 throughput ceiling (125MB/s) is hit during scheduled analytics scans, stalling writes fleet-wide (~40 pods crash-looping ~10x/hour for 14+ days). He has a concrete one-command fix ready but lacks the IAM permission to run it, and flagged the same risk exists on 35+ other ClickHouse volumes fleet-wide. Patrik and five senior engineers joined the channel the same moment this was posted.
key_events: 17 Jul 14:34 UTC status moved to Monitoring; Andrea's 'next week' check-in point set

20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo posts a new, deeper root-cause finding: the h2bet ClickHouse 16TB gp3 volume is capped at default 125MB/s throughput; scheduled Hex analytics scans pin disk reads at that ceiling for 5+ min, writes collapse, and the clickhouse-writer fleet (~40 pods) times out and crash-loops together — happening ~10x/hour for 14+ days
20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo proposes a one-command live fix (AWS EC2 modify-volume, ~$15/mo) but flags he can't run it himself — his SOC role lacks ec2:ModifyVolume

20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo flags this is fleet-wide: 35+ ClickHouse volumes across eu1/eu2/betconstruct/wt-ltk/everymatrix clusters share the same gp3 default-throughput risk (incl. roobet 12.9TB, gamdom 4.9TB, padisahbet 4.4TB) — h2bet is just the first to become symptomatic
20 Jul 13:36 CEST Patrik Potocki, Stephen Riolo, Max Holdaway, Agustín Castaño, Curt Healey and Andrew Pirotta all join the channel
proposals: Schema/type-mismatch fix (ActionEvent.Status int vs string) — still not implemented, ownership handed to Matteo as of 17 Jul
open_breaches: A4/D12: a 14+ day recurring crash-loop is now root-caused with a ready one-line fix, but execution is blocked purely on an IAM permission gap — needs a same-day decision, not silent drift, especially given the fleet-wide exposure Matteo flagged (35+ volumes)
synthesis.why: 🟡 — this is very fresh (posted ~1h before this digest run) so a decision hasn't had time to land yet, but it's an important emerging pattern: a long-standing (14+ day) recurring problem now has a concrete, cheap, ready fix that's purely blocked on access, plus a fleet-wide risk across 35+ volumes that no one has acted on yet. Patrik and several senior engineers are already in the channel — worth confirming next run that the h2bet fix was applied and that someone owns reviewing the other volumes.
synthesis.suggested_action: Grant Matteo (or have someone with access run) the ec2:ModifyVolume fix for h2bet today; assign an owner to review throughput settings across the other 34 similarly-provisioned ClickHouse volumes flagged fleet-wide.
